title: ranunculus dissectus subsp. sibthorpii
intro: Ranunculus dissectus subsp. sibthorpii, also known as the Lesser Celandine or Pilewort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids and saponins. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as acting as a natural diuretic. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus dissectus subsp. sibthorpii include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
